mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to expected equity cash flows; increases in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months predict lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.33 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is above -0.03 in absolute failure direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real activity in retail sales indicates resilient household demand and supports near-term revenue expectations; positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 703088 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of walk-forward observations or the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening labor income and recession risk; when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.1 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation combined with restrictive policy tightens financial conditions; when CPIAUCSL inflation is above target while FEDFUNDS is elevated, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 313.049 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33 in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition CPIAUCSL YoY inflation > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 5% produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading cyclical demand indicator; when HOUST crosses below 1300 thousand units, weaker construction activity predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1277.0 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 1300 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production reflects stronger output volumes and operating leverage; positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through cyclical earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.1168 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion accompanied by stable unemployment signals non-inflationary income growth; when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise over 3 months, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 158638.0 and UNRATE was 4.1 in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-stable condition is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth supports aggregate earnings expectations; when GDPC1 growth over the latest available quarter is positive, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 22758.752 as of the latest available quarterly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or if triggered average returns are not above unconditional returns.
